data.epi_content:
"Miss, well. Just think of it as experiencing childhood.

You're small anyway, so you have more space in there."

At those words, all the children around start snickering.

Whether to humor Lydia or because they genuinely find it funny.

Even in this cramped situation, I tried moving around to somehow get out.

The door opens slightly when I push.

And in front of me, Lydia is staring at me with an expressionless face.

She closes the door and starts banging on it forcefully.

"Uh...ugh."

The inside starts ringing madly, and I feel like I'm about to retch.

My stomach churns violently.

Click, snap.

And in that gap, I hear the sound of something being locked from outside the locker.

"Let me out."

No answer comes.

Footsteps fade away.

"Please let me out."

I try raising my voice, but it remains faint.

I wonder if my voice even echoed inside the locker.

This is worse than last time.

Last time it was just a wardrobe.

The space was wide enough to lie down.

I touch my cheek, wondering if this will make her torment me more severely.

I don't know. Is she just bullying me because she wants to?

I don't remember doing anything bad to Lydia.

I never looked down on her, just lived normally.

Clearly there was a hierarchy, a vertical relationship, but I never did anything to Lydia.

I did go with those friends to ask Vivian to stay away from Evan, trying to create an intimidating atmosphere, but nothing else.

So why is she doing something this cruel?

I'm surrounded on all sides, wanting to breathe but struggling to do so.

Forcibly hugging my knees, I breathe with difficulty, unable to move.

I want to stretch my legs but can't.

I want to spread my arms but can't.

If I open my mouth to speak, the sound echoes in this narrow space.

It's terrible.

This just isn't right.

I'm not Crown Prince Sado, why should I be confined in a locker like a rice chest?

I'm neither rice nor a book, so please let me out.

"Let me out."

I'm starting to suffocate.

The words I utter are now little more than mumbles.

"Let...hnngh...me out."

Rather than wanting to cry, the tears just burst out first.

I just feel sad.

"Uuuu...sniff...uuu."

I don't want to cry.

But the tears come anyway.

I feel like I'm going crazy.

I just hate being confined.

But I'm trapped in this space where I can't move in this uncomfortable position, can't see ahead, and where sounds echo when I make noise.

If I could fall asleep, I could at least pass the time, but this subtly uncomfortable position prevents even sleep.

When I close my eyes trying to escape, my tingling body reminds me of reality.

"I don't know what I did wrong, but I'm sorry, I apologize."

No answer comes.
data.epi_content2:
Because there's no one outside.

Because I can't sense anyone's presence.

"So please let me out."

Nevertheless, I continue to convey my request.

Desperately begging to someone who likely isn't there.

Finally giving up, I lower my head again after having raised it slightly.

How many minutes have passed?

No clock, no light.

And my forcibly bent legs and crouched position make it feel like blood isn't circulating in my body.

It's not just a feeling—it's reality.

In this dark space, time seems to stretch out, feeling like it's gnawing at my mind.

And my life too.

"Save me."

My face must be a mess.

Tears, snot, and sweat all over, with disheveled hair sticking here and there.

I've been trying to ignore the rising smell of urine, but that doesn't change the fact that I wet myself out of fear of the dark.

Surely air must be coming in through tiny gaps, but even that seems scarce as I gradually feel like I'm not breathing even when I am.

It's like this space is filled with carbon dioxide.

I've never stayed this long in a damp, smelly, dirty space before.

Ah, I should exclude the experience of dying while submerged in my own blood, if my blood counts as filth.

"Let me out of here."

What should I do when I get out?

Yes, I'll grab a pistol first and blow Lydia's head off before blowing my own.

Then I'll blow the head off that boy who grabbed my arm so I couldn't escape, and while the children are in shock, I'll randomly shoot at the kids Lydia treats like her minions.

I'm starting to feel somewhat grateful for my privilege that's become almost useless now.

Unlike other children, I don't have to worry about being caught with a pistol in the Academy.

And most people just look the other way.

Maids don't dare question what's in a student's room.

Commoners don't scrutinize nobles' possessions, lest they face terrible consequences.

If they're accused of theft, that maid's ordinary life is essentially over.

After crying for a while, my throat gets dry.

Full of ice...

"I want to drink coffee."

Even if I close my eyes and open them again, the dark scene before me doesn't disappear.

I'm always aware that this isn't a nightmare but reality, yet it remains terribly awful.

"Let...me...out."

I just keep repeating the plea to let me out while crying.

Because if I don't say these words, it feels like no one will come.

Because it feels like I'll slowly wither and die trapped in this narrow space.

Though not much time has passed, it feels like a day or two has gone by.

"Damn it, let me out, this... this isn't right."

Being trapped in a locker, unable to move, unable to use force to get out—this helplessness makes this lady abandon all notions of dignity, manners, etiquette, and propriety, causing her to cry, show emotion, and spout profanities.

Well, if you take someone who abhors insects and starve them for days, wouldn't they eat insect dishes?

Not quite the same context, but still.

"Let me ouuuuut!!! Aaaaaaah!! Aaaaaaah!"

Thud. Thud, thud.

I knocked on the door desperately.

Whether trying to open it or just flailing, I'm not sure.

My head buzzes maddeningly, and I grow increasingly irritable.

So I screamed with a voice that could barely come out properly.

Though in this confined space, the sound just echoes.

Thump. Thump.

When I shook my whole body, the locker began to move back and forth.

Screaming didn't relieve my emotions at all.

Rather, it made me more exhausted, deranged, with despair rising to my chin.

So, just.

I kept repeating the plea to let me out, then crying, catching my breath when tired, then repeating the plea again.

Even if my voice became scratchy from my sore throat.
data.epi_content3:
Knock knock.

While I was sobbing for a long time, I heard a knocking sound.

"Um, is someone trapped in there?"

I quickly wiped away my tears and straightened my hair as best I could.

Who could it be?

At least it's not Lydia's voice.

But what if Lydia sent them?

If I answer wrong here, she might keep me locked in here for hours more.

If she doesn't like my response and keeps me confined until sunset and sunrise again.

I'd go crazy.

I'm scared.

Nevertheless, thinking I might be able to get out of here, I answer immediately.

"I'm trapped, please let me out."

With a slightly trembling voice, I said those words.

Then with a clicking sound, the lock that kept the locker door from opening breaks.

The door opens simultaneously.

But having been crouched in this position for so long, my body is stiff and can't even think about getting out.

When I try to extend my arm for help and look up at the face of my savior, I'm momentarily speechless.

"E...Erica...?"

Vivian helped me.

Of all people, Vivian—whom I didn't want to show this pathetic state—helped me.

Suppressing the rising feelings of distress, I somehow managed to wiggle my body out of the locker as if nothing was wrong.

Then I collapse with a thud.

I tried to stand up by putting strength in my legs, but they simply wouldn't respond.

Instead, as blood suddenly started flowing to my legs, they began to tingle madly.

But this lady never shows such weaknesses in front of others.

Unable to move because my legs are tingling, yet sitting on the floor as if nothing's wrong—wouldn't Vivian find this amusing?

Anyway, to break this awkward atmosphere, I first express my gratitude.

"Thank you, Vivian."

"Are...are you okay? No, you were trapped in here, who on earth..."

"And about slapping your cheek before, I'm really sorry."

"This isn't the time for that."

Vivian approaches to support me.

Even though I must smell terrible after being stuck in there without washing or using the bathroom.

I push her away with difficulty and stand up again on my trembling legs.

Then I start walking unsteadily, supporting myself on desks and walls.

"So, just leave me alone. Please.

I want to run to the rooftop and jump off right now."

"What's that suddenly..."

"Leave me alone! Just alone! Aaaah!!"

As my legs gave out, a surge of irritation washed over me.

After frantically pulling at my snow-white hair, I gradually regain my senses.

"Ah, I'm sorry. Right now, I'm just, just having a hard time.

Having a hard time, is it okay to be like this when it's hard, or not, I don't know. I'm sorry."

Vivian's eyes shake wildly as she looks at me.

She doesn't seem to understand.

But I didn't have enough energy to explain it to her.

I'm just tired.

Whatever it is.

"Because it's hard."

I walk toward my room, muttering those words repeatedly.

To a place that may not be safe, but is cozy.
